mnemonics.title: PWD Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: PWD = Present Working Directory - Remember: 'Print Where's Daddy' to recall that pwd tells you where you are.

mnemonics.title: CD Navigation Pattern
mnemonics.content: CD: Change Directory - Use: cd [path]. Remember: Think of CD as 'Change Direction'.

mnemonics.title: Dot Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: . = Here (current), .. = Up there (parent). Single dot points to self, double dot points to parent - like eyes looking at self and then up.

mnemonics.title: Tilde Home Reminder
mnemonics.content: ~ (tilde) is above Tab key - like going HOME above where you are. Think: Tilde ~ looks like a little house roof.

acronyms.usage: Command to display current directory path. Essential for verifying your location in the file system.
Command to navigate between directories. Most frequently used shell command for file system navigation.
Command to list directory contents. Often used with options like -l (long), -a (all), -h (human-readable).
Command to create new directories. Use -p flag for creating nested directories.
Command to remove empty directories. Use rm -r for non-empty directories.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree starting from root (/). Absolute is like giving full address (Country>State>City>Street>House). Relative is like saying 'two streets down from here'.
Imagine an inverted tree with root at top. / is trunk, branches are /home, /var, /etc. Files are leaves. .. moves up one branch, . stays on same branch.
Think of path resolution like reading directions: Absolute says 'Start from city center (root) and go to X'. Relative says 'From here (current), go to X'.

analogies.analogy: Like a full postal address: House No., Street, City, State, Country - complete from start to end.
Like giving directions from your current location: 'Walk two blocks north, then turn right' - depends on where you are now.
The foundation of a building - everything else is built upon it. Cannot go higher than root.
The floor above you in a building. .. takes you up one floor, ../../ takes you up two floors.
The room you are currently standing in. . refers to this very room.
